name: Illinois Currency Exchange
description_short: Multi-service currency exchange in downtown Chicago offering check cashing, money orders, wire transfers, vehicle registration, and notary services with extended hours.
description_long:
New Chicago Rush Currency Exchange, Inc. is a full-service currency exchange and financial services center located at 62 E Chicago Ave. in Chicago's downtown Loop area. The company has been operating as part of the Currency Exchange Association, positioning itself as an alternative to traditional banks for consumers seeking convenient access to multiple financial services in a single location.

The company offers a comprehensive range of services spanning check cashing, money orders, wire transfers, ATM access, foreign currency exchange, bill payment, and notary services. Beyond traditional currency exchange functions, they provide vehicle-related services including auto title transfers, license plate renewals, and registration assistance directly connected to Illinois Secretary of State systems. Additional offerings include tax preparation, coin counting, prepaid cards, postage stamps, private mailboxes, and government document retrieval (birth, marriage, death certificates).

New Chicago Rush distinguishes itself through extended operating hours (7 AM–10 PM Monday–Saturday, 9 AM–5 PM Sunday), positioning itself as more accessible than traditional banks. The company emphasizes one-stop convenience, allowing customers to handle check cashing, vehicle registration, bill payment, and notary needs without visiting multiple locations. Their direct integration with Illinois Secretary of State systems for vehicle registration enables same-transaction license plate issuance.

However, this company is fundamentally a check-cashing and currency exchange service, not a lender or credit provider. Consumers seeking personal loans, emergency cash advances, or credit-building products will need to look elsewhere. The broad service menu, while convenient, suggests a retail-focused operation rather than depth in any single financial service category. No information is publicly available regarding fee structures, competitive rates, or customer reviews.
pros: Extended hours: Open 7 AM–10 PM Monday–Saturday, providing access outside standard bank hours
One-stop convenience: Handles check cashing, money orders, vehicle registration, notary, bill pay, and tax prep in one location
Direct DMV integration: Real-time connection to Illinois Secretary of State for immediate license plate issuance
Comprehensive service menu: Offers 30+ services including foreign currency exchange, prepaid cards, postage, and mailbox rental
Downtown accessibility: Located at 62 E Chicago Ave. in Loop, easily accessible by public transit
Government services: Can issue Cook County birth/marriage/death certificates and handle city vehicle sticker renewals
Multiple contact methods: Phone, fax, and physical location available for customer service
cons: No fee transparency: Website provides no information on check cashing fees, foreign exchange rates, or service charges
Not a lending institution: Cannot provide personal loans, emergency cash, or credit products
Limited digital presence: No online services, mobile app, or ability to conduct transactions remotely
No customer reviews or ratings: Website provides no third-party validation of service quality or customer satisfaction
Service complexity: Some services require specific documentation; customers must call ahead to confirm requirements
best_for: Unbanked and underbanked consumers needing check cashing without a bank account
Chicago residents requiring vehicle registration, license plate renewal, or title transfer services
Customers needing multiple financial services (check cashing, bill pay, notary, tax prep) in one location
People working irregular hours who need access to financial services outside standard bank hours
diagnosis: New Chicago Rush Currency Exchange is best for unbanked and underbanked Chicago residents who need check cashing, money orders, or vehicle registration services and value extended hours and multi-service convenience. The main caveat is that this is a service provider, not a lender—it cannot help with credit building, emergency loans, or debt management.
